rulings.Case Title: ATHOS EQUIPMENT D'ESCRIME, LLC VS. ALEXEY KUZNETSOV et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Complete A Discovery Proceeding Or, In The Alternative, To Reopen Discovery
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 17, 2012, Line PLAINTIFF ATHOS EQUIPMENT D'ESCRIME, LLC Notice Of Motion And Motion To Complete A Discovery Proceeding Or, In The Alternative, To Reopen Discovery Pro Tem Judge Mark Schreiber, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: No opposition filed. Plaintiff's motion is granted. Pursuant to CCP Section 2024.050(a), plaintiffs are granted leave to complete the discovery proceeding requested, the motion for discovery sanctions against defendants. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to mschreiber@cwclaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative rulin

rulings.Case Title: ATHOS EQUIPMENT D'ESCRIME, LLC VS. ALEXEY KUZNETSOV et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Discovery Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 17, 2012, Line 1, PLAINTIFF ATHOS EQUIPMENT D'ESCRIME, LLC Notice Of Motion And Motion For Discovery Sanctions, Pro Tem Judge Mark Schreiber, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: No opposition filed. Plaintiff's motion is granted as against Defendants Alexey Kuznetsov and and Olessia Kuznetsov as follows: The facts designated in the following subdivisions of Section III(c) of the Memorandum of Points and Authorities in Support of the Motion for Discovery Sanctions shall be taken as established in this action: Subdivision i -- a, b, c, d, e, f, g, h, i, j, k, l, m, n, o Subdivision ii -- a, b, c, d, e, f Subdivision iii -- a, b, c Subdivision iv -- a, b, c Subdivision v -- a, b, c, d Subdivision vi -- a, b, c

rulings.Case Title: ATHOS EQUIPMENT D'ESCRIME, LLC VS. ALEXEY KUZNETSOV et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Athos Equipment E'Escrime, Llc'S Motion For Discovery Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 17, 2012, Line 1, PART 2 OF TENTATIVE RULING - Plaintiff Athos Equipment E'Escrime, Llc'S Motion For Discovery Sanctions - The court strikes all affirmative defenses of Defendants Alexey Kuznetsov and Olessia Kuznetsov from the Answer to the Second Amended Complaint. Monetary sanctions are imposed against Defendants Alexey Kuznetsov and Olessia Kuznetsov and awarded to Plaintiff in the amount of $6,420. The monetary sanctions shall be paid within fifteen days of notice of this order. Plaintiff shall provide a proposed order setting forth at length the subdivisions of Section III(c) of the Memorandum of Points and Authorities describing the issue sanctions described above. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to mschreiber@cwclaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: LARRY JACOBS VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To Vacate The Hearing For Mtn Summary Judgment/Adjudication; Memo Of P & A And Req For Judicial Ntc
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, August 17, 2012, Line 4, PLAINTIFF LARRY JACOBS' Motion To Vacate The Hearing For Motion Summary Judgment/Adjudication. The motions to vacate are granted. The hearing dates of September 14 and September 20 for the summary judgment motion are vacated because defendant's notices failed to comply with CCP 437c. See Robinson v. Woods (2008) 168 Cal.App.4th 1258; McMahon v. Sup. Crt. (2003) 106 Cal.App.4th 112. Defendant's remedy is to seek a trial continuance and leave to calendar the motion for summary judgment within the 30-day period prior to trial if necessary. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION, INC., DBA GREAT VS. SAKHUJA FAMILY HOLDINGS, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To Compel Answer To Post-Judgment Discovery And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions; Memo Of P & A; Dec And Pos
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 17, 2012, Line 3, PLAINTIFF CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION, INC., DBA GREAT Ntc Of Mtn To Compel Answer To Post-Judgment Discovery And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Mark Schreiber, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: No opposition filed. The motion is granted. The defendants SAKHUJA FAMILY HOLDINGS, LLC; SAKHUJA FAMILY HOLDINGS-MARIPOSA, LLC; SAKHUJA FAMILY HOLDINGS II, LLC; DIGITAL PREPRESS INTERNATIONAL, INC. AND SANJAY SAKHUJA, INDIVIDUALLY DBA DIGITAL PREPRESS INTERNATIONAL shall each furnish responses to Plaintiff's Post Judgment Interrogatories and Plaintiff's Post Judgment Demands for Production of Documents and Tangible Things within fifteen days of notice of this order. Further, the defendants shall pay monetary sanctions in the amount of $340 to plaintiff within fifteen days of notice of this order.

rulings.Case Title: RACHAEL HORLAK VS. KIMPTON HOTELS AND RESTAURANTS GROUP LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, August 17, 2012, Line 18, DEFENDANT KIMPTON HOTELS AND RESTAURANTS GROUP LLC's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained in part. The demurrer to the third and fifth causes of action are sustained with ten days leave to amend. As to the third cause of action, Plaintiff requested leave to amend in order to assert an additional remedy. As to the fifth cause of action, the covenant of good faith and fair dealings only protects benefits incorporated in the specific terms of the contract. Plaintiff is granted leave to amend, if she can do so in good faith, to allege the contract provisions, or facts supporting implied-in-fact contract provisions, that entitled Plaintiff to a benefit that Defendant violated. The demurrer is overruled as to the second cause of action, the conditions of Plaintiff's employment were materially altered when she was asked to leave her position and not come to work. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
